text: Graphics are often used to summarize large quantities of data that are contained within scientific publications. While there are many different applications that can be used to create graphics, the creation and use of graphics can be limited by such things as programming skills, costs to purchase and learn applications and by platform compatibility. In this study, we have developed a freely accessible online web server named SRplot that provides an interface to more than a hundred of the most commonly used data visualization and graphing functions. The web server can be easily used with all Web browsers and no special computing ability is required for the users’ machines. The SRplot web server has a graphical user-friendly interface (GUI) allowing users to simply copy and paste the data from their input file into the specific fields within the GUI provided in the web server, which can easily edit and plot the graphics on the fly. The plots created by SRplot can be easily exported to publication quality bitmap (PNG/TIFF) and vector graphics formats (PDF/SVG). The SRplot web server is a dynamic online application that is constantly being updated and expanded. The functions within SRplot are being continually improved, modified and optimized as well as being updated based on the valuable feedback and suggestions from users. To date, numerous plots have been created by SRplot and have been published in over 500 peer-reviewed scientific journals. The SRplot web server is available at the following URL: http://www.bioinformatics.com.cn/SRplot .
explanation: The text uses generic promotional language with limited technical detail, a pattern often seen in AI-generated summaries, though it could also be a real product description.
